w:r.w:delText: The Development Lab strives to be a holistic community and “one-stop shop” that incorporates access to 4 main pillars: high-end, sport-specific training, speed/agility training, tutoring services, and a central focus on social-emotional development through sports psychological therapy. The youth sports industry is currently valued at roughly $40 billion today, and is projected to reach $70 billion by the year 2030. The goal is to create something that is scalable, and can be used across all different sports and geographic areas.

w:r.w:t: The Development Lab is a youth athlete development platform designed to help families find and engage trusted providers across four core pillars: sport-specific training, speed and agility development, tutoring, and mental performance support. Rather than forcing families to rely on fragmented referrals and disconnected research, the platform aims to create one place to discover, evaluate, and connect with the right specialists. With the youth sports market projected to grow meaningfully through 2030, the long-term opportunity is to build a scalable marketplace that can expand across sports, age groups, and geographies.

w:r.w:delText: Create a platform that can provide individualized services (i.e. training only, tutoring only, etc.), or a comprehensive package that includes access to all services on a monthly basis.

w:r.w:t: Deliver both standalone services and bundled memberships so families can engage a single provider or build a more complete athlete development plan through one platform.

w:r.w:delText: Connect families, youth programs, school programs, or club programs with a list of providers and services that best meet their overall needs and budget.

w:r.w:t: Help families, schools, clubs, and youth programs quickly identify best-fit providers based on needs, budget, geography, and development goals.
Problems We Are Looking to Solve
Numerous providers in saturated markets make it hard for trainers/tutors to get continued business, or they have to waste resources trying to market for new clients.
We will bring customers/clients to providers through our matchmaking algorithm.
Families have to rely on word of mouth, research, and networking to find the best resources and specialists for their children.
We will create a “one-stop shop” that mitigates stress and saves families time to find the resources that they need.
New York Market Space and Projection

w:r.w:delText: Initial Capital (roughly $25-$75k)

w:r.w:t: Securing enough initial capital to fund branding, MVP development, legal setup, and early customer acquisition (estimated $25K-$75K).

w:r.w:delText: Building brand, technology stack, securing domains and LLC

w:r.w:t: Building brand credibility, launching the technology stack, and formalizing the business infrastructure.

w:r.w:delText: Securing Specialists to create initial database during pilot phase

w:r.w:t: Recruiting enough high-quality specialists to create a compelling supply base during the pilot phase.
Integrated App/Website Design that houses specialists in all areas and price point ranges
Targets a family’s specific budget and wants/needs to “match” them with a list of providers that fit their search criteria.
Utilizing advertising as additional revenue stream
Replace GitHub-issue style lead capture with a credible inquiry flow, response SLA, and owned follow-up process.
Add provider vetting, safety, privacy, consent, and liability language that is explicit about minors and licensed services.
Define the full operating flow after a match: inquiry, response, consultation, booking, payment, cancellation, refund, and dispute handling.
Clarify premium-tier value inside the product so providers know exactly what paid visibility, analytics, and sponsorship include.
Show real price anchors or sample packages so families and programs can compare options without guessing what budget tiers mean.
Replace raw provider lists with validation status so investors can see who is prospective, engaged, pilot-ready, or committed.
Add proof points the MVP must earn before outreach scales: provider activation, inquiry-to-booking conversion, retention, CAC, and payback period.

w:r.w:delText: Possible Revenue Streams (this is where more time needs to be focused)

w:r.w:t: Primary revenue streams for launch and early scale

w:r.w:delText: Contract to youth programs, club programs, and school athletic programs

w:r.w:t: Program partnerships with youth programs, clubs, and school athletic departments

w:r.w:delText: Free participation (little effort and downside from a program’s perspective)

w:r.w:t: Low-friction pilot participation to drive early adoption, testimonials, and case studies

w:r.w:delText: Database of specialists in various fields

w:r.w:t: Specialist marketplace with free basic listings and paid premium visibility

w:r.w:delText: Free registration, 50% per new client paid to TDL

w:r.w:t: Free registration paired with a percentage fee on new-client revenue sourced through TDL

w:r.w:delText: Two-tiered subscription system that is correlated to “sponsored” results

w:r.w:t: Tiered subscription packages tied to visibility, sponsorship placement, and lead volume
Ad Space for website and app

w:r.w:delText: Pricing Model for Launch?
Basic Listing - Free
Commission on New Clients - 15%

w:r.w:t: Founding Provider - $49/month for a limited founding cohort

w:r.w:delText: Premium Specialist Tier - $99/month
Founding Specialist Offer - $49/month for life
Program Partnerships - $1,000/annually

w:r.w:t: Transaction Fee (Phase 2 only) - 10% of bookings processed through TDL

w:r.w:delText: App/website that houses specialists in the following areas:

w:r.w:t: Static MVP and curated provider-directory concept spanning the following areas:

w:r.w:delText: Social-Emotional Development (child/adolescent psychologists)

w:r.w:t: Mental Performance Support and referral pathways to licensed clinicians when appropriate
Families will fill out a registration page / survey that includes:

w:r.w:delText: Dynamic Matchmaking algorithm

w:r.w:t: Rules-based matching engine in the MVP

w:r.w:delText: Proprietary algorithm

w:r.w:t: Matching logic to refine with pilot data
Contracting with specialists to have them included in search results, allowing them to connect with more families and build their brands
Specialists will include their geographical location and pricing
Pay content specialists to provide videos to build up a self-help database

w:r.w:delText: Validate that families and specialists will actively use the platform.

w:r.w:t: Validate that families will submit intake, specialists will engage with outreach, and both sides will respond to a lightweight matching workflow.

w:r.w:delText: MVP (Lean Version)
Features:
Booking inquiry system

w:r.w:t: Inquiry capture and manual handoff workflow

w:r.w:delText: Admin dashboard
Payments: Stripe

w:r.w:t: Payments (future phase): Stripe Connect or equivalent

w:r.w:delText: Differentiate through proprietary technology.

w:r.w:t: Differentiate, if validated, through workflow quality, trusted supply, and data gathered during pilots.

w:r.w:delText: This is where The Development Lab becomes difficult to copy.

w:r.w:t: This could become more defensible over time if the company earns repeat usage, provider trust, and proprietary data.

w:r.w:delText: Become the dominant youth athlete development platform.

w:r.w:t: Build a trusted regional foothold and a repeatable pilot model before broader expansion.

w:r.w:delText: “LinkedIn + Headspace + Hudl + Airbnb for Athlete Development”

w:r.w:t: Trusted athlete-support marketplace for focused regional markets

w:r.w:delText: List of Known Providers/Specialists

w:r.w:t: DAWG - Brendan Homire and Quintin Germain

w:r.w:delText: My name is Tim Rau, and I’m the founder of The Development Lab — a new athlete development platform focused on supporting student-athletes through a more holistic approach centered around Mind • Body • Game.

w:r.w:t: My name is Tim Rau, and I’m the founder of The Development Lab — a platform designed to support student-athletes through a more holistic model built around Mind, Body, and Game.

w:r.w:delText: As both an educator and someone deeply connected to youth development, I’ve seen how fragmented the current landscape can feel for families trying to find trusted specialists across training, academics, mental performance, wellness, and recruiting.

w:r.w:t: As both an educator and someone deeply connected to youth development, I’ve seen how difficult it can be for families to find trusted support across training, academics, and mental performance.
We’re currently speaking with a select group of highly respected specialists on Long Island to better understand:
how families are currently finding support,
and how a trusted developmental network could better serve both athletes and providers.

w:r.w:delText: Your background and reputation made me feel you’d be incredibly valuable to speak with.

w:r.w:t: Given your background and reputation, I believe your perspective would be especially valuable as we shape the pilot.

w:r.w:delText: This is very much still in the early relationship-building and research phase, and I’d genuinely love to hear your perspective and experiences.

w:r.w:t: We’re still in the early research and relationship-building phase, and I’d genuinely value the chance to learn from your experience.

w:r.w:delText: Would you be open to a quick 15–20 minute conversation sometime over the next couple weeks?

w:r.w:t: Would you be open to a brief 15–20 minute conversation sometime in the next couple of weeks?
Predicated on building trust…we are here to build relationships and that’s how we will succeed.
Listen to their story, learn more about why they do what they do and see if it fits our mission and values
Understand Pain Points - great leading questions:
How do families usually find you?
What challenges do you face growing your business?
What frustrations do you experience operationally?
What do you wish families better understood?
Validate our vision and introduce our mission and core values
Find that Collaborative Buy-In - express to specialists that we are looking to build something that actually supports professionals and families in a way that is symbiotically beneficial for everyone
Sign, Seal, Deliver - keep it light to keep guard down
Are you potentially interested in staying involved as we continue to shape this pilot and grow?
